name: USS Abraham Lincoln
area: Arabian Sea — blockade enforcement and Project Freedom support (USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4)
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.
mission_summary: Primary strike platform — blockade enforcement and Hormuz mine clearance support; position unchanged Apr 27
notes: Day 68 (May 6): Lincoln UNCHANGED — Northern Arabian Sea, co-located with George H.W. Bush CSG (CONFIRMED Fleet Tracker May 4). Operation Epic Fury 'concluded' per SecState Rubio (May 5 White House briefing); Trump paused Project Freedom evening May 5 per Truth Social citing Pakistani-mediated MOU progress; US naval blockade of Iranian ports REMAINS active. CSG count CENTCOM stable at 2 (Lincoln + Bush, Northern Arabian Sea co-located) + Tripoli LHA-7 ARG (Arabian Sea, with USS New Orleans LPD-18 + USS Rushmore LSD-47, 31st MEU embarked). Boxer ARG (USS Comstock LSD-45 + USS Portland LPD-27, 11th MEU) Indian Ocean AIS-dark following Malacca transit May 1; ETA CENTCOM ~May 11 per TWZ. Ford CSG westbound transit toward Norfolk (Suez northbound May 1 confirmed); 314-day record-setting deployment ending. NEW ASSETS: USS Pioneer (MCM-9) and USS Chief (MCM-14) Avenger-class minesweepers from Sasebo Japan approaching CENTCOM AOR per Fleet Tracker May 4 — specifically relevant given Iranian sea-mine threat in Strait of Hormuz. DESRON 21 with Lincoln: USS Spruance (DDG-111). Independently deployed in 5th Fleet AOR: USS Milius (DDG-69, Yokosuka), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119, Norfolk). Force posture is consistent with 'deal-or-pause' not 'deal-or-resume-major-strikes' in the next week — adding the Boxer ARG by mid-May would mark capability inflection. Day 67 (May 5): Lincoln UNCHANGED — Northern Arabian Sea, supporting Operation Epic Fury / blockade / PROJECT FREEDOM Day 2. Pentagon briefing (Hegseth/Caine May 5) confirmed 'ceasefire is not over'; Caine: Iran has attacked US forces 10+ times, commercial vessels 9 times, seized 2 container ships since ceasefire — 'all below threshold of restarting major combat operations.' 22,500 mariners on 1,550+ vessels stuck in Persian Gulf; six other ships attempting to violate Iranian-port blockade 'were all turned around.' New CENTCOM force additions per USNI May 4: USS Pioneer (MCM-9) and USS Chief (MCM-14) Avenger-class minesweepers approaching from Sasebo for Hormuz mine clearing. CSG count CENTCOM stable at 2 (Lincoln + Bush, both Northern Arabian Sea) + Tripoli LHA-7 ARG; Boxer ARG entered Indian Ocean May 1. Day 66 (May 4): Lincoln UNCHANGED — Arabian Sea, supporting Operation Epic Fury / blockade / PROJECT FREEDOM (USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4). DESRON 21 escorts: USS Spruance (DDG-111), USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121). CVW-9 embarked (VFA-14, VFA-41, VFA-151, VMFA-314, VAQ-133, VAW-117). Co-located with USS George H.W. Bush CSG (CVN-77) in Arabian Sea — first time two CSGs together in CENTCOM since war start. CSG count CENTCOM 3→2 (Ford departing). Day 66 milestone: PROJECT FREEDOM launched — CENTCOM commenced 'guiding' US-flagged commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz; force package included 2 USN guided-missile destroyers (likely from Lincoln/Bush DESRON pool plus independents) entering Persian Gulf alongside 100+ aircraft and 15,000 service members. US Navy SANK 6 IRGC SMALL BOATS while facilitating passage of 2 US-flagged merchant vessels. CENTCOM denied Iranian Fars News claim US warship struck near Jask. CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirects: 50 vessels (+2 vs Day 65 of 48). Day 65 (May 3): Lincoln remains on station in Arabian Sea — confirmed conducting U.S. blockade operations per USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 27 and U.S. Navy photo. Iran ceasefire holds Day 25 (no kinetic strikes since 8 PM ET Apr 7). CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirections: 48 vessels (CBS quoting CENTCOM May 2; +3 in past 20 hours; +3 vs Day 64 baseline 45). Iran condemns U.S. capture of Majestic X and Tifani as 'armed robbery on the high seas' (FM spokesperson Baqaei). Iran's 14-pt counter-proposal delivered via Pakistan May 2; Trump 'cannot imagine' acceptable; IRGC May 3 — 'impossible military operation or a bad deal.' Per manifest 4c, DESRON 21 escorts include USS Spruance (DDG-111); independents support blockade — McFaul, Milius, Delbert D. Black, Pinckney, Mitscher, Frank E. Petersen Jr., Michael Murphy, John Finn. Sunday-data caveat: USNI Fleet Tracker last refreshed Apr 27 (typically published Mondays); next refresh expected Mon May 4. Day 64 (May 2): Lincoln continues operating in northern Arabian Sea — blockade enforcement; Iran ceasefire holds Day 24 (no kinetic strikes since 8 PM ET Apr 7). CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirections: 45 vessels (ABC News live blog/CENTCOM May 1, +3 vs Apr 29 baseline 42). NO new vessel-boarding incidents Day 63-64. CSG-3 / CVW-9 (VMFA-314 F-35Cs) embarked. DESRON 21 with USS Spruance (DDG-111) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112). Per manifest 4c, Lincoln CSG escorts also include USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121), USS Mitscher (DDG-57) Persian Gulf near Hormuz, USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS John Finn (DDG-113), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pickney (DDG-91), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Tulsa (LCS-16) Bahrain. Trump May 1 letter to Hill asserts hostilities 'terminated'; Senate WPR vote 50-47 (6th failure, Collins crossed). IRGC Aerospace Cdr Mousavi (May 2): war resumption 'likely' (rhetorical). 3-CSG posture (Lincoln+Ford+Bush) holds — first time since OIF 2003. Day 63 (May 1): Lincoln continues operating in northern Arabian Sea — blockade enforcement; no reposition reported. USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker most-recent publication remains April 27, 2026; SOF News Apr 30 'Epic Fury Update' confirms Lincoln in Arabian Sea / blockade ops. CENTCOM (Cooper Apr 29 HASC) cumulative blockade redirections 42 vessels (unchanged through May 1). No specific new May 1 vessel-boarding incident verified. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 10 of extension / Day 23 overall — zero strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7). MAY 1 WAR POWERS 60-DAY DEADLINE: Senior administration official (AP) said hostilities 'have terminated' for WPR purposes; Hegseth (SASC Apr 30) said clock 'pauses or stops' under ceasefire — two competing admin theories in tension. Senate rejected Apr 30 War Powers resolution (Sen. Susan Collins joined Democrats). Sen. Murkowski threatens AUMF when Senate returns week of May 11. Adm. Cooper briefed Trump Apr 30 on next-phase options including 'limited ground interventions, targeted strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure' (SOF News). CENTCOM Apr 29 Dark Eagle hypersonic-weapon AOR request remains pending. Iran transmitted updated peace proposal via Pakistani mediators May 1 (IRNA) — Brent settled $108.10 (-5.4%), WTI $101.05 (-4.1%); Trump reaffirmed blockade continues 'until [Tehran] cries uncle.' Day 62 (Apr 30): Lincoln remained on station northern Arabian Sea — Brent intraday 4-year high $126.41 (Asia) before paring on Axios CENTCOM strike-options report. AAA gas $4.30 (4-year high). Day 61 (Apr 29): Lincoln continues operating in northern Arabian Sea, enforcing naval blockade of Iranian ports. NO new carrier movement reported in the Apr 28-29 window — USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker most-recent publication remains April 27, 2026 (no Apr 29 weekly issue); TWZ Carrier Tracker most-recent April 26, 2026. APR 28 BLOCKADE ENFORCEMENT: 31st MEU Marines (operating with Tripoli ARG alongside Lincoln CSG) rappelled from helicopter to board Comoros-flagged container ship M/V Blue Star III in the Arabian Sea on suspicion of breaching the U.S. naval blockade — released after CENTCOM determined no Iranian port call was planned; CENTCOM disclosed cumulative redirected vessels now 39 (up from 34, +5 in window) and >20 ships now moored/anchored at Iran's Chah Bahar port (vs. ~5/day pre-blockade baseline). Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 8 of extension / Day 21 overall — zero strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7); Bushehr NPP at ~25 consecutive days no incident. APR 29: Trump in Axios phone interview rejected Iran's Hormuz-for-blockade-lift proposal — 'They are choking like a stuffed pig'; Hegseth/Caine/Hurst first HASC testimony since war began (Hurst pegged cumulative cost ~$25B). Tripoli ARG continues operating in Arabian Sea alongside Lincoln CSG (TWZ Apr 26). Day 60 (Apr 28): Day 59 (Apr 27): Lincoln continues operating in northern Arabian Sea, enforcing naval blockade of Iranian ports (TWZ Carrier Tracker Apr 26). USNI News Fleet Tracker page Apr 27 returned 403 on direct fetch; cached snippet confirms continued deployment with VFA-14, VFA-41, VFA-151, VMFA-314 (F-35C) and VAQ-133 air wing. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 6 of extension / Day 19 overall — zero strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7). Iran transmitted written proposal via Pakistani mediators offering Hormuz-for-blockade-lift in exchange for ceasefire extension and DEFERRED nuclear talks (Axios primary Apr 27); Trump convened Situation Room meeting; said proposal 'much better' but Iran 'offered a lot but not enough.' Simultaneous IRGC Telegram counter: Hormuz control is 'definitive strategy'; Deputy Speaker Nikzad cited Mojtaba Khamenei order Hormuz 'under no circumstances' returns. AP Apr 27: 'U.S. says it's clearing Iranian mines in latest push to open the Strait of Hormuz' — follow-up to Trump's 'shoot and kill' order. Anomaly: Mordashov-linked superyacht NORD transited Hormuz Saturday (Bloomberg). Day 58 (Apr 26): Lincoln remains forward-most CSG in the northern Arabian Sea — no reposition reported Apr 25-26 (USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 20 baseline; Apr 27 tracker not yet published). Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 6 of extension / Day 19 overall — zero strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7). Trump CANCELLED Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan trip Apr 25 ('we have all the cards; they have none! If they want to talk, all they have to do is call'); reverted to phone-only diplomacy. Iran FM Araghchi cycled Islamabad → Muscat (met Sultan Haitham) → back to Islamabad → bound for Moscow Apr 27 to meet Putin. Apr 24-25: USS Pinckney (DDG-91) helicopter intercepted sanctioned shadow-fleet tanker M/V Sevan in Arabian Sea, redirected toward Iran; USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) intercepted Iranian-flagged ship attempting to reach Iranian port (CENTCOM photo released Apr 25). CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirects: 37 vessels since Apr 13 commencement (+M/V Sevan added). Hegseth (Apr 24): 'Our blockade is growing and going global. No one sails from the Strait of Hormuz to anywhere in the world without the permission of the United States Navy.' Lebanon front actively deteriorating: IDF Sgt. Idan Fooks (19) killed by Hezbollah explosive drone — third IDF KIA since Apr 17 truce. UK PM Starmer-Trump phone call Apr 26 urged Hormuz reopening. War Powers May 1 deadline 5 days remaining. Day 57 (Apr 25): Lincoln remains forward-most CSG in the northern Arabian Sea — blockade enforcement continuing per CENTCOM photo Apr 16 and USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 20 (Apr 27 tracker not yet published as of Day 57); no new reposition reported. CSG-3 / CVW-9 embarked (VMFA-314 F-35Cs). DESRON 21 with USS Spruance (DDG-111) embarked; independently deployed DDGs in region: USS Milius (DDG-69, Yokosuka), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pickney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), USS John Finn (DDG-113), USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115); ADC: USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121). Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 4 of extension / Day 18 overall — zero strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7); Bushehr NPP at 21 consecutive days no new incident. Hegseth (Apr 24): blockade 'as long as it takes'; second carrier (Bush) joining 'in coming days.' CENTCOM/Navy Times Apr 24: 34 commercial vessels redirected since Apr 13. CENTCOM Apr 24 image (per CNN): U.S. destroyer pictured intercepting an Iranian-flagged vessel near the Strait of Hormuz. Apr 25: Iranian FM Araghchi delivered 'list of demands' to Pakistani officials in Islamabad and departed for Muscat then Moscow; White House announced Witkoff/Kushner Pakistan trip but CNN reports arrival slipped to Sunday Apr 26. No new vessel boardings or seizures reported in the Apr 25 24h window; M/V 'Yuri' supertanker (2M bbl Iranian crude from Kharg) remained halted near Larak Island after starting Hormuz transit late Thursday. Day 56: Lincoln remains forward-most CSG for Epic Fury blockade enforcement in the northern Arabian Sea — anchor of the 3-CSG posture announced by CENTCOM Apr 24 (first time 3 U.S. carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since Operation Iraqi Freedom 2003). CSG-3 with CVW-9 embarked (including VMFA-314 F-35Cs). DESRON 21 with USS Spruance (DDG-111), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), and USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121, Air Defense Cmdr). Combined 3-CSG order of battle in CENTCOM: >200 aircraft, 15,000 sailors/Marines, 9+ DDGs. Iran ceasefire holds (Day 17, no kinetic strikes on Iran since 8 PM ET Apr 7); INDEFINITE extension announced by Trump Apr 21. Defense Sec. Hegseth (Apr 24): blockade will continue 'as long as it takes.' CENTCOM/Navy Times Apr 24: 34 commercial vessels redirected since Apr 13 (SOF News cites 33). Apr 24: Iranian FM Araghchi flew to Islamabad; Witkoff/Kushner depart for Pakistan Apr 25. Pentagon (Apr 22 briefing to Congress): Hormuz mine clearance could take ~6 months. Day 55: Lincoln remains forward-most CSG for Epic Fury blockade enforcement in the northern Arabian Sea (USNI News Fleet Tracker Apr 20 remains latest published; no Day 55 update). CSG-3 with CVW-9 embarked (including VMFA-314 F-35Cs). DESRON 21 with USS Spruance (DDG-111), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), and USS Frank E. Petersen Jr. (DDG-121 as ADC). APR 23 ROE ESCALATION: Trump ordered US Navy via Truth Social to 'shoot and kill any boat' laying mines in Strait of Hormuz — most significant rules-of-engagement change since Apr 13 blockade; Pentagon boarded another 'sanctioned stateless vessel' carrying Iranian oil in Indian Ocean overnight Apr 22-23; Reuters reports ≥3 Iranian-flagged tankers intercepted in Asian waters (redirected from near India, Malaysia, Sri Lanka). CENTCOM Apr 23: 31 vessels turned back since Apr 13 (up from 28 Apr 21); ZERO have broken through; operation now involves 10,000+ soldiers, 17 warships, 100+ aircraft. Gen. Caine (JCS Chairman) Apr 23 reaffirmed Adm. Paparo (INDOPACOM) will 'actively pursue any Iranian-flagged vessel or any vessel attempting to provide material support to Iran'; noted 'finite levels' of munitions being expended. Apr 22 Hormuz escalation carried over: IRGC fired on 3 commercial vessels, SEIZED MSC Francesca and Epaminondas. Trump EXTENDED Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY Apr 21 pending Iranian 'unified proposal' while maintaining blockade. Islamabad Round 2 collapsed Apr 22. US Senate defeated Baldwin war-powers resolution 55-46 Apr 23 (5th failed attempt).
source: USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (May 4), CENTCOM @CENTCOM May 4, Adm. Brad Cooper press conference May 4, Hegseth/Caine Pentagon briefing (May 5), Rubio White House briefing May 5, Trump Truth Social May 5/6, CENTCOM Apr 24 announcement, CNN, USNI News, TWZ Carrier Tracker May 3

name: USS Gerald R. Ford
area: DEPARTING CENTCOM AOR — Transited Suez Canal NORTHBOUND May 1, en route Norfolk; deployment 314+ days (record-setting, exceeds 295-day post-Vietnam record set by Lincoln in 2020)
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets (CVW-8), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
status: DEPARTING CENTCOM AOR — NORTHBOUND TO NORFOLK
mission_summary: Transited Suez Canal NORTHBOUND May 1 (Washington Post via TWZ); 'reportedly begun the journey back home to Norfolk.' Deployment 314+ days — record-setting (exceeds 295-day Lincoln post-Vietnam record), approaches Vietnam-era 300-day-plus norms. Effective REMOVAL from CENTCOM AOR as of May 2-4. CSG count CENTCOM 3→2 (Lincoln + Bush remain).
notes: Day 66 (May 4): FORD CSG DEPARTING CENTCOM — Confirmed transit of Suez Canal NORTHBOUND May 1 (Washington Post via TWZ; USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4). 'Reportedly begun the journey back home to Norfolk' after 314+ days deployed (record-setting, exceeds Lincoln 2020 post-Vietnam record of 295 days). Effective REMOVAL from CENTCOM AOR as of May 2-4. DESRON 2 (Ford CSG) sailing northbound w/ Ford toward Norfolk — USS Bainbridge (DDG-96), USS Mahan (DDG-72), USS Winston S. Churchill (DDG-81). CSG count CENTCOM 3→2 (Lincoln + Bush remain in Arabian Sea co-located; Tripoli ARG also Arabian Sea). Ford's transit out is the principal force-disposition change since prior horizon. Pentagon settling in for protracted blockade-plus-Project Freedom posture rather than positioning for resumption of major Iran strikes — consistent with Trump's calibrated RoE choice (rejected Cooper's 'forced-entry' plan Apr 30). Day 65 (May 3): Per manifest 4a / discrepancy #1 — USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 27 (and Apr 20) places Ford CSG in EASTERN MEDITERRANEAN SEA after returning to Med post Apr 11 Suez transit. Internal dashboard 'Red Sea / routine flight ops' label appears stale; USNI is gold-standard. Per Apr 27 tracker, escorts Bainbridge (DDG-96) + Thomas Hudner (DDG-116) reportedly remained in Red Sea (split deployment). Iran ceasefire holds Day 25. CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirected vessels = 48 (CBS via CENTCOM May 2). USNI Fleet Tracker next refresh expected Mon May 4. CSG-12 / CVW-8 embarked; DESRON 2 — Mahan (DDG-72). Eastern Med independent DDGs per manifest: USS Bulkeley (DDG-84), USS Gonzalez (DDG-66), USS Oscar Austin (DDG-79). Day 64 (May 2): Per manifest 4a — USNI News Fleet Tracker (Apr 27 most-recent edition, May 4 not yet posted) places Ford in Red Sea conducting 'routine flight operations' per CENTCOM Apr 29 statement; transited Suez southbound Apr 16. Iran ceasefire holds Day 24. SOF News Apr 30 reported homeward-transit intent remains UNVERIFIED by Navy/USNI; carry forward Red Sea position with MEDIUM confidence. CSG-12 / CVW-8 embarked. DESRON 2 escorts in Red Sea per manifest: USS Mahan (DDG-72), USS Bainbridge (DDG-96), USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116). Eastern Med independent DDGs: USS Bulkeley (DDG-84), USS Gonzalez (DDG-66), USS Oscar Austin (DDG-79). 3-CSG posture (Lincoln+Ford+Bush) holds — first time since OIF 2003. Day 63 (May 1): SOF News (Apr 30) 'Epic Fury Update' reports USS Gerald R. Ford 'will depart the Middle East in the next few days' after a deployment exceeding 10 months — REPORTED INTENT, NOT YET CONFIRMED by Navy/USNI; no observed Suez/Gibraltar transit in open-source ship spotting. Treating area as 'Eastern Med — pending homeward transit' pending verification; if departure executes, downstream removal of Ford strike group from CENTCOM-AOR-tagged set expected early-to-mid May. CSG-12 / CVW-8 embarked (DESRON 2 — Mahan, Bainbridge, Hudner, Churchill). Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 10 / Day 23). MAY 1 WAR POWERS 60-DAY DEADLINE: Senior administration official (AP) said hostilities 'have terminated' for WPR purposes; Hegseth (SASC Apr 30) says clock 'pauses or stops.' Senate rejected Apr 30 WPR resolution (Collins defects). Iran transmitted updated peace proposal via Pakistan mediators May 1 (IRNA); Brent −5% to $108.10. Cooper Apr 30 briefed Trump on next-phase options including 'limited ground interventions, targeted strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure.' Day 62 (Apr 30): Ford on station (last cleanly verified position Apr 26-27 northern Red Sea per TWZ/USNI); Brent intraday $126.41 high (Asia). Day 61 (Apr 29): No carrier movement changes confirmed in the Apr 28-29 window — USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker most-recent April 27, 2026; TWZ Carrier Tracker most-recent April 26, 2026. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 8 of extension / Day 21 overall). Day 60 (Apr 28): Day 59 (Apr 27): Ford CSG location DISCREPANCY pending verification — USNI News Apr 27 snippet (referencing Apr 11 imagery) suggests Mediterranean Sea operations, while TWZ Carrier Tracker Apr 26 places CSG in northern Red Sea after Apr 16 Suez southbound transit. Possible CSG repositioning back northward, or USNI snippet using older photo. FLAG — verify next cycle via fresh USNI fetch or AP shipping reports. Component of CENTCOM-announced 3-CSG posture (Lincoln + Ford + Bush) — first time three U.S. carriers in Middle East simultaneously since 2003 OIF holds. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 6 / Day 19); Iran transmitted written Hormuz-for-blockade-lift proposal via Pakistani mediators (Axios primary Apr 27); Trump convened Situation Room. IRGC simultaneously declared Hormuz control 'definitive strategy.' Lebanon ceasefire DEADLIEST DAY since truce inception: 14 killed (incl. 2 women, 2 children) and 37 wounded in southern Lebanon strikes Apr 26; one IDF KIA in southern Lebanon combat (cumulative IDF KIA Lebanon now 16); 6 IDF wounded; IDF intercepted 3 drones inbound from Lebanon; Netanyahu: 'Hezbollah's violations are, in practice, dismantling the ceasefire.' IDF ordered evacuations of 7 villages north of Litani Apr 26-27. Lebanese MoPH cumulative 2,521 / 7,750. Houthi pause continues post-Apr 8. Day 58 (Apr 26): Ford CSG continuing Red Sea operations — no reposition Apr 25-26 (USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 20 baseline; Apr 27 not yet published). CVW-8 embarked. Component of CENTCOM-announced 3-CSG posture (Lincoln + Ford + Bush) — first time three U.S. carriers in Middle East simultaneously since 2003 OIF (~23 years). Lebanon ceasefire actively fraying: IDF Sgt. Idan Fooks (19) killed by Hezbollah explosive drone Apr 26 — third IDF KIA since Apr 17 truce; Netanyahu publicly declared Hezbollah was 'disintegrating' the ceasefire ('we have eliminated 46 terrorists'). IDF overnight airstrikes on Hezbollah rocket launchers at Deir El Zahrani, Reman, Al-Saamiya, Nabatiye al-Faouqa killed 6 (AFP photo confirmed). Lebanese MoPH cumulative above 2,500 killed (consolidated 2,502) / 7,725 wounded. Houthi pause continues post-Apr 8 — Ford's southern Red Sea positioning retains sea-based strike reach without sitting in the highest-threat cone. UK PM Starmer-Trump phone call Apr 26 urged Hormuz reopening (first publicly confirmed UK-US Iran call of ceasefire phase). Day 57 (Apr 25): Ford CSG continuing operations in the Red Sea after southbound Suez transit Apr 16 (USNI Fleet Tracker Apr 20 baseline; TWZ Apr 20). CVW-8 embarked. DESRON 2 escorts: USS Mahan (DDG-72) embarked; Red Sea — USS Bainbridge (DDG-96), USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116); E. Med independents — USS Bulkeley (DDG-84), USS Gonzalez (DDG-66). Component of the CENTCOM-announced 3-CSG posture (Lincoln + Ford + Bush) — first time three U.S. carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since OIF 2003. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 4 of extension / Day 18 overall). Israel-Lebanon truce extended 3 weeks Apr 23 but actively violated: Apr 25 Israeli strikes killed 4 in Yohmor al-Shaqeef (Lebanese MoPH); IDF intercepted a 'suspicious aerial target' in southern Lebanon; Hezbollah lawmaker Ali Fayyad called the extension 'meaningless.' Houthi activity remains absent post-Apr-7 — Ford's southern Red Sea positioning retains sea-based strike reach without sitting in the highest-threat cone. Day 56: Ford CSG in central Red Sea — over 300 days deployed (longest post-Cold War US carrier deployment, per Stars & Stripes Apr 24). CVW-8 embarked. Per CENTCOM/USNI News, current Ford CSG escorts include USS Mahan (DDG-72) and USS Winston S. Churchill (DDG-81), in addition to USS Bainbridge (DDG-96) and USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116). Component of the 3-CSG posture announced by CENTCOM Apr 24 (Lincoln + Ford + Bush) — first time three carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since OIF 2003. Iran ceasefire holds (Day 17). Lebanon ceasefire EXTENDED 3 weeks (Trump-brokered Apr 23) but mutual violations confirmed: IDF killed 6 Hezbollah fighters in Bint Jbeil + 2 civilians in Touline Apr 24; Hezbollah shot down Israeli drone, attacked IDF APC. Israeli DefMin Katz (Apr 23): Israel 'awaiting a green light' to resume war and 'return Iran to the dark ages.' Day 55: Ford CSG in central Red Sea off Jeddah — transited Suez southbound Apr 16 (USNI News, TWZ; USNI Apr 20 remains latest tracker). CVW-8 embarked (VFA-31, VFA-37, VFA-87, VFA-213; VAQ-142; VAW-124). DESRON 2 with USS Bainbridge (DDG-96), USS Mahan (DDG-72), plus USS Thomas Hudner (DDG-116). Ford deployment past record 300+ days at sea — longest post-Cold War US carrier deployment. Three-carrier posture: Lincoln (northern Arabian Sea, blockade enforcement), Ford (Red Sea), Bush (transiting Horn of Africa). Trump EXTENDED Iran ceasefire INDEFINITELY ~16:22 ET Apr 21 pending Iranian 'unified proposal'; naval blockade maintained. Islamabad Round 2 collapsed Apr 22. APR 23: Trump ordered 'shoot-and-kill' ROE on Hormuz mine-laying boats (Truth Social); Pentagon boarded another 'sanctioned stateless vessel' overnight; CENTCOM blockade tally up to 31 vessels turned back. Lebanon-Israel Round 2 ambassadors' talks convened in Washington Apr 23 (moved from State Dept to White House; US added Amb. Mike Huckabee; Israel's Yechiel Leiter, Lebanon's Nada Hamadeh Moawad; Rubio expected to join part). IDF Commando Brigade killed 2 armed Hezbollah operatives near Aynata/Bint Jbeil Apr 23. Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon killed Al-Akhbar journalist Amal Khalil (PM Salam: 'war crime'). Houthi activity absent through Apr 23 per corpus — Ford's southern Red Sea positioning retains sea-based strike reach without sitting in the highest-threat cone.
source: USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (May 4 — Suez northbound Apr 30/May 1 confirmed), Washington Post via TWZ 'Where Are The Carriers' May 3, Stars & Stripes, SOF News 'Epic Fury Update' Apr 30, CENTCOM Apr 24 announcement

name: USS George H.W. Bush
area: Arabian Sea — co-located with USS Abraham Lincoln CSG (first time two CSGs together in CENTCOM since war start) (USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4)
aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk
status: ARABIAN SEA — CO-LOCATED WITH LINCOLN CSG
mission_summary: Operating in Arabian Sea co-located with USS Abraham Lincoln CSG (first time two CSGs together in CENTCOM since war start). DESRON 22 embarked: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Ross (DDG-71); USNS Arctic (T-AOE-8) fast oiler. CVW-7 (Jolly Rogers VFA-103, Rampagers VFA-83, et al.).
notes: Day 66 (May 4): Bush UNCHANGED — Arabian Sea, co-located with USS Lincoln CSG (first time two CSGs together in CENTCOM since war start) per USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4. CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked (Jolly Rogers VFA-103, Rampagers VFA-83, et al.). DESRON 22 embarked: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Ross (DDG-71). USNS Arctic (T-AOE-8) fast oiler accompanies. Bush entered CENTCOM Apr 23 after rounding Africa; now anchor of remaining 2-CSG posture (Lincoln + Bush) following Ford departure (Suez northbound May 1). Bush + Lincoln + Tripoli ARG configuration is what the Navy can realistically sustain for months — Pentagon settling in for protracted blockade-plus-Project Freedom posture. Day 66 force action: 2 USN guided-missile destroyers entered Persian Gulf May 4 in support of Project Freedom (specific hulls not yet released in CENTCOM statements; likely from above pool plus DESRON 21 elements). Day 65 (May 3): Per manifest 4a / USNI Apr 27 — Bush off coast of Africa, reportedly heading to join 5th Fleet buildup. Iran ceasefire holds Day 25. Position confidence: MEDIUM. CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked. DESRON 22 escorts: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71); USS Mason (DDG-87) deployed Mar 24 from Mayport. 3-CSG posture (Lincoln+Ford+Bush) holds — analyst note: if Bush fully checks into 5th Fleet, dashboard should treat as escalation indicator. CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirected vessels = 48 (CBS via CENTCOM May 2). Sunday-data caveat: USNI Fleet Tracker last refreshed Apr 27; next expected Mon May 4. Day 64 (May 2): Per manifest 4a / USNI Apr 27 — Bush transiting Horn of Africa en route to 5th Fleet (TWZ unable to independently confirm via open source). Position confidence: MEDIUM. Iran ceasefire holds Day 24. CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked. DESRON 22 escorts per manifest: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71), USS Mason (DDG-87). 3-CSG posture (Lincoln+Ford+Bush) holds — first time since OIF 2003 — but structurally unsustainable per analyst notes (Lincoln longest-deployed; Ford sustained Mar 12 fire repaired Souda Bay). Pentagon May 1 announced ~5,000 troop drawdown from Germany over 6-12 months — adjacent force-flow signal. Day 63 (May 1): No new authoritative position published since USNI Apr 27 (which placed Bush 'off coast of Africa transiting to 5th Fleet'); SOF News Apr 30 references continued CENTCOM 3-CSG posture. Best estimate: Bush approaching Bab-el-Mandeb / northern Red Sea per estimated transit from Apr 6 USNI report and average ship speed. Position confidence: MEDIUM. CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked. DESRON 22 escorts: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71); USS Mason (DDG-87) deployed Mar 24 from Mayport. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 10 / Day 23). MAY 1 WAR POWERS 60-DAY DEADLINE: Senior administration official (AP) said hostilities 'have terminated' for WPR purposes; Hegseth (SASC Apr 30) says clock 'pauses or stops.' Senate rejected Apr 30 WPR resolution (Collins defects). Iran transmitted updated peace proposal via Pakistan mediators May 1 (IRNA); Brent −5% to $108.10. Adm. Cooper briefed Trump Apr 30 on next-phase options including 'limited ground interventions, targeted strikes on Iranian energy infrastructure' (SOF News). Day 62 (Apr 30): Bush continued transit through CENTCOM AOR; Brent intraday $126.41 high (Asia). Day 61 (Apr 29): No carrier movement changes confirmed in the Apr 28-29 window — USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker most-recent April 27, 2026; TWZ Carrier Tracker most-recent April 26, 2026. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 8 of extension / Day 21 overall). Day 60 (Apr 28): Day 59 (Apr 27): Bush CSG operating in U.S. 5th Fleet AOR (CENTCOM); CENTCOM Apr 25 (X) confirmed CSG sailed in Indian Ocean Apr 23 and arrived in 5th Fleet last week (TWZ Carrier Tracker Apr 26). First time since 2003 Iraq War with three U.S. carriers in CENTCOM simultaneously — still holds. CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked. DESRON 22 escorts: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71); USS Mason (DDG-87) deployed Mar 24 from Mayport. No confirmed escort composition changes Apr 27. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 6 / Day 19); Iran transmitted written Hormuz-for-blockade-lift proposal via Pakistani mediators (Axios primary); Trump convened Situation Room; said proposal 'much better' but 'not enough.' Simultaneous IRGC counter-signal: Hormuz control 'definitive strategy.' AP Apr 27 headline: U.S. clearing Iranian mines in Hormuz. Mordashov-linked superyacht NORD transited Hormuz Saturday (Bloomberg) — anomaly. Day 58 (Apr 26): Bush CSG continues operations in CENTCOM AOR (Indian Ocean) en route to operational station — Bush CSG arrival publicly confirmed by CENTCOM Apr 24; second carrier joining blockade per Hegseth Apr 24. First time three U.S. carriers in Middle East simultaneously since 2003 OIF (~23 years). Avenger-class minesweepers USS Chief and USS Pioneer reportedly transiting Pacific from Japan toward CENTCOM (per Pentagon Apr ~24); not yet in theater. CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked. Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 6 of extension / Day 19 overall — zero strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7). Trump CANCELLED Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan envoy mission Apr 25; reverted to phone diplomacy. CENTCOM cumulative blockade redirects rose to 37 vessels (+M/V Sevan intercepted by USS Pinckney heli Apr 24-25). Position confidence: HIGH — CENTCOM/Hegseth confirmation Apr 24. Day 57 (Apr 25): Bush CSG continues operations in CENTCOM AOR — likely Red Sea or Gulf of Aden by Apr 25. USNI homepage tease (Apr 24) read 'East Coast carrier USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) is now operating in...' (article body not publicly indexed at time of writing). TWZ Apr 20: CSG was transiting around the Horn of Africa toward the Middle East. Hegseth Apr 24 Pentagon briefing: 'A second U.S. aircraft carrier will join the blockade in the coming days.' Position confidence: MEDIUM — pending USNI Apr 27 fleet tracker (PROBABLE — pending USNI confirmation). CSG-10 / CVW-7 embarked. DESRON 22 escorts: USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71); USS Mason (DDG-87) deployed Mar 24 from Mayport. Pentagon told Congress (Apr 22) clearing Hormuz of Iranian mines could take ~6 months even after hostilities end (DIA assessment 1-6 months). Iran indefinite ceasefire holds (Day 4 of extension / Day 18 overall); blockade continues; 34 vessels redirected (CENTCOM Apr 24). Apr 25: no new vessel boardings or seizures reported. Day 56: BUSH CSG NOW IN CENTCOM AOR — formally announced by CENTCOM via X on Apr 23 (after transiting around southern Africa via Cape Agulhas / Mozambique Channel route, avoiding Bab el-Mandeb). With escorts USS Mason (DDG-87), USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Ross (DDG-71) and DESRON 22 staff; fast oiler USNS Arctic (T-AOE-8). Bush's arrival completes the 3-CSG posture announced by CENTCOM Apr 24 — first time three U.S. carriers operate simultaneously in the Middle East since Operation Iraqi Freedom (2003). Combined 3-CSG order of battle: >200 aircraft, 15,000 sailors/Marines. CSG-10 with CVW-7 embarked, commanded by RADM Alexis Walker. Departed Norfolk Mar 31. Per Pentagon, Avenger-class mine-hunters (pair) departed Japan and were last reported leaving Sri Lanka 'a few days ago' transiting toward CENTCOM (SOF News Apr 24); only confirmed mine-sweeper currently in region: USS Canberra (LCS-30, Independence-class). Pentagon told Congress (Apr 22) clearing Hormuz of Iranian mines could take ~6 months even after hostilities end (DIA assessment 1-6 months). Iran ceasefire holds (Day 17); blockade continues; 34 vessels redirected (CENTCOM Apr 24).
source: USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (May 4), CENTCOM Apr 25 (X), TWZ Carrier Tracker Apr 26 + 'Where Are The Carriers' May 3, Hegseth Pentagon briefing (Apr 24)
position_confidence: HIGH — USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4 + CENTCOM confirmation

name: USS Tripoli
strike_group: Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group / 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: CENTCOM AOR — Arabian Sea — operational with F-35Bs, ~3,500 personnel
aircraft: F-35B Lightning II (VMFA-121), MV-22B Osprey (VMM-265), AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53K King Stallion
mission_summary: Rapid-response force for Strait of Hormuz — littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, maritime security, potential island seizure operations
notes: Day 66 (May 4): Tripoli UNCHANGED — Arabian Sea (USNI Fleet & Marine Tracker May 4); 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines, ex-Okinawa) embarked with F-35Bs (VMFA-121 'Green Knights'), MV-22B (VMM-265), MH-60S (HSC-25). May 2: Adm. Cooper visited USS Milius (DDG-69) — signaled visible CENTCOM-commander engagement with surface combatants ahead of Project Freedom Day 66 launch. NOTE: USS Boxer (LHD-4) ARG transited Strait of Malacca Apr 30, entered Indian Ocean May 1, dark on AIS, est. arrival CENTCOM 'sometime next week' (per TWZ); not yet in CENTCOM AOR as of May 4. Day 65 (May 3): USS Tripoli (LHA-7) operates in Arabian Sea (Gulf of Oman adjacent) — 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines, ex-Okinawa) embarked with F-35Bs (VMFA-121 'Green Knights'), MV-22B (VMM-265), MH-60S (HSC-25). MAY 2: CENTCOM Cdr Adm. Brad Cooper visited Sailors and Marines aboard the America-class LHA in the Arabian Sea, recognized top performers, toured spaces (@CENTCOM X post 2026-05-02). Visit timed to same day Iran delivered 14-pt counter-proposal via Pakistan — strategic-communication signal of 3-CSG-plus-LHA concentration as deterrent visual. Tripoli arrived AOR 2026-03-28; primary blockade-enforcement role. Day 53: Tripoli ARG in Arabian Sea; 31st MEU Marines from this force conducted the Apr 19 Touska boarding (rappelled from two MH-60S Seahawks off Tripoli for VBSS). 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines/sailors) embarked, ~20 F-35Bs (VMFA-121) — Tripoli operates as 'Lightning Carrier.' Tripoli ARG (LHA-7 + LPD-18 USS New Orleans + LSD-47 USS Rushmore + 31st MEU) remains forward-positioned for continuing blockade enforcement, littoral warfare, mine countermeasures, potential island seizure ops. USS Boxer (LHD-4) ARG + 11th MEU transiting toward CENTCOM (last confirmed near Guam Apr 11, INDOPACOM AOR). Ceasefire expiry pushed to ~20:00 ET Wed Apr 22. Iran demanded Touska's immediate release Apr 21; Iran FM Baghaei called seizure 'maritime piracy' and a 'war crime.' US delegation (Vance, Witkoff, Kushner) en route to Islamabad for Round 2 talks Apr 22; Iranian attendance unconfirmed EOD Apr 21.
source: USNI Fleet and Marine Tracker (May 4), @CENTCOM X 2026-05-02 (Cooper Tripoli visit), 2026-05-02 (Cooper Milius visit), CENTCOM statement on Touska seizure (Apr 19), CNN/CNBC/Axios/Jerusalem Post/Aviationist

name: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron
area: Bahrain / Strait of Hormuz — conducting mine countermeasures
mission_summary: Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz from Bahrain
notes: Day 37: Three LCS conducting MCM from Bahrain. CRITICAL GAP: Iran possesses estimated 5,000-6,000 naval mines. Last four Avenger-class minesweepers in Bahrain decommissioned Sep 2025. Only 3 LCS with MCM mission packages available; Pentagon OT&E confirmed no operational testing of LCS MCM package in FY2025. Experts estimate clearing Hormuz requires 16 MCM vessels; Navy has 7 total (4 Avengers in Japan). CENTCOM destroyed 44+ minelayers.
source: USNI Fleet Tracker, CRS Report, CENTCOM, UK MoD, Bloomberg, Apr 5 reporting

name: USS Florida
area: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.
source: CENTCOM statements, classification prevents specific confirmation

name: USS John Finn
area: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
mission_summary: Air defense, escort operations, strike support
notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.
detachment_note: Operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen in Arabian Sea

name: USS Boxer
strike_group: Boxer Amphibious Ready Group / 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit
area: Indian Ocean — entered May 1 after transiting Strait of Malacca Apr 30; dark on AIS; ETA CENTCOM AOR early-to-mid May (per TWZ 'Where Are The Carriers')
aircraft: AV-8B Harrier II or F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Osprey, AH-1Z Viper, UH-1Y Venom, CH-53E Super Stallion
mission_summary: 11th MEU embarked. Transited Strait of Malacca Apr 30, entered Indian Ocean May 1, dark on AIS. Estimated arrival CENTCOM 'sometime next week' per TWZ. Provides amphibious depth following Ford CSG departure; will sustain Lincoln + Bush + Tripoli ARG + Boxer ARG configuration as protracted blockade-plus-Project Freedom posture.
notes: Day 66 (May 4): Boxer ARG transited Strait of Malacca Apr 30, entered Indian Ocean May 1, dark on AIS, est. arrival CENTCOM 'sometime next week' per TWZ 'Where Are The Carriers' May 3 — not yet in CENTCOM AOR as of May 4. Provides amphibious depth following Ford CSG departure (Suez northbound May 1). Boxer carries ~2,500 Marines from Camp Pendleton. Day 54: Boxer ARG with 11th MEU transiting the Pacific westbound near Guam en route to CENTCOM AOR per manifest Apr 22 (USNI). Pentagon indicated Boxer would either relieve Tripoli's Asia tasking or follow it to the Middle East.
source: TWZ 'Where Are The Carriers' May 3, USNI Fleet Tracker, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon

name: HMS Anson
mission_summary: UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
notes: Day 29: Royal Navy Astute-class SSN. Departed Perth March 6. Tomahawk-armed. Northern Arabian Sea (reported). No official UK confirmation of exact position.

name: 82nd Airborne Division
area: CENTCOM AOR — undisclosed Middle East locations — 1st BCT 'Devil Brigade' (~2,000 paratroopers)
mission_summary: Ground combat force — 1st BCT Immediate Response Force forward-deployed; speculated mission sets include Kharg Island seizure or Strait of Hormuz operations
notes: Day 44: ~2,000 paratroopers from 1st BCT (Immediate Response Force) remain forward-deployed to undisclosed Middle East locations since March 24-25. ~50,000+ US service members participated in Operation Epic Fury. Forces remain deployed — no drawdown. Islamabad talks collapsed Apr 12 after 21 hours; Trump declared US Navy will blockade Hormuz. 82nd Airborne postured for contingencies if ceasefire collapses after Apr 22 expiration. 60-day War Powers deadline Apr 29. Congress returns Apr 14.
source: Reuters, Stars and Stripes, Pentagon, Apr 12 reporting

name: HMS Dragon
notes: Day 29: Type 45 destroyer. Departed Portsmouth March 10 for air defense mission in Eastern Mediterranean.

name: FS Charles de Gaulle
area: Eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay — expanded multinational escort including French, Italian, Spanish, Dutch, German vessels
mission_summary: French carrier operations — Eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay. France NOT participating offensively in Epic Fury but has shot down Iranian drones 'in legitimate defense'
notes: Day 41: Charles de Gaulle remains in the eastern Mediterranean near Crete/Souda Bay with an expanded multinational escort including French, Italian, Spanish, Dutch, and German vessels. France is NOT participating offensively in Epic Fury but has shot down Iranian drones 'in legitimate defense.' Macron announced 8 additional frigates and 2 Mistral-class amphibious ships deployed to the region on March 9. French leadership has demanded Lebanon's inclusion in the ceasefire — Macron stated Lebanon was 'fully included.'
source: French MoD / Reuters / Macron statement / Apr 9 reporting

name: VMFA-311 Marine F-35Cs
area: RAF Lakenheath, UK — staging en route to CENTCOM
aircraft: F-35C Lightning II (first 5 of 10 arrived Mar 23-24; first-ever land-based F-35C combat deployment)
mission_summary: Marine F-35C squadron staging through UK en route to CENTCOM theater
notes: Day 27: The War Zone confirmed Mar 24 that first 5 of 10 planned F-35Cs from VMFA-311 'Tomcats' arrived RAF Lakenheath Mar 23-24, having departed MCAS Miramar Mar 10. First-ever land-based combat deployment of the F-35C. Wikipedia states all 10 arrived; second batch of 5 UNVERIFIED in primary reporting. Also notable: AC-130J Ghostrider gunship arrived Lakenheath from Iceland Mar 24.
source: The War Zone Mar 24, USNI News, Pentagon

name: Bundeswehr Frankenthal-class minesweeper + command/supply ship
area: Mediterranean Sea — pre-positioning for potential Hormuz mine-clearance mission
mission_summary: Pre-positioned in Mediterranean to save transit time pending any future Bundestag mandate for German participation in a multinational Hormuz mine-clearance mission
notes: Day 57 (Apr 25): Defence Minister Boris Pistorius (interview to Rheinische Post) announced the Bundeswehr will pre-position one Frankenthal-class minesweeper plus a command-and-supply ship in the Mediterranean ahead of any Bundestag mandate for a Hormuz mine-clearance mission. Conditions for Hormuz deployment: (a) lasting ceasefire, (b) international-law framework, (c) Bundestag mandate. Germany has 10 Frankenthal-class hulls but realistically only 3-4 deployable; transit time to Hormuz estimated 5 weeks (German TV Apr 23). Chancellor Merz had previously said Germany is ready to participate in a multinational Hormuz mission, preferably with U.S. involvement. NOT a deployed combat asset — flag clearly as pre-positioning policy signal.
source: Al Arabiya Apr 25 [https://english.alarabiya.net/News/world/2026/04/25/germany-deploys-minesweeper-to-mediterranean-for-possible-hormuz-mission-defense-minister], The Local DE Apr 25 [https://www.thelocal.de/20260425/germany-to-despatch-minesweeper-for-possible-hormuz-mission], anews.com.tr Apr 25

name: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower
area: Norfolk, VA — completed sea trials Apr 24 following 15-month maintenance availability; not deployed
mission_summary: Completed sea trials Apr 24, 2026 following 15-month maintenance availability; status: ready for tasking, post-availability work-up; not yet deployed
notes: USNI / TWZ Apr 26: completed sea trials Apr 24 after 15-month maintenance period; available as strategic reserve / potential rotation reinforcement for CENTCOM if Iran ceasefire collapses
source: USNI News Fleet & Marine Tracker; TWZ Carrier Tracker Apr 26

notes: TWZ Apr 20: departed San Diego Apr 15 for 3rd Fleet AOR routine ops/training; reportedly outfitted with new Raytheon Coyote 8-cell counter-UAS launcher
